message:
feat(models): pi parity — resolve bare *_API_KEY values as env-var references (issue #64)

The original `pi` CLI lets users put a bare environment variable *name* in
`models.json` (e.g. `"apiKey": "DASHSCOPE_API_KEY"`) and resolves it to the
real secret at runtime. Without this, `pi_agent_rust` would treat the name
as a literal key and the provider auth would silently 401.

This change adds opt-in env-var indirection for values that look like an
upper-snake-case env var name ending in the literal suffix `_API_KEY`,
while keeping literal keys (`sk-ant-...`, `sk-...`) working unchanged.

Implementation:
- New `looks_like_api_key_env_var(value)` predicate. Conservative: requires
  uppercase ASCII letters/digits/underscores, must start with a letter, must
  end with the literal suffix `_API_KEY`, and the prefix must be non-empty
  (so the bare string `_API_KEY` is rejected). Real provider keys do not
  match this shape, so the heuristic is safe.
- New `resolve_value_with_resolvers(value, base_dir, env_lookup)` accepts an
  injected env lookup closure. The crate forbids `unsafe`, so tests cannot
  use `std::env::set_var`; this seam lets unit tests exercise every branch
  without mutating process-wide env state. The existing
  `resolve_value_with_base` now delegates to this with `std::env::var`.
- When `looks_like_api_key_env_var(value)` matches:
    * env var set + non-empty → return the trimmed env value.
    * env var set but empty/whitespace → emit a `tracing::warn!` and fall
      back to the literal value.
    * env var unset → emit a `tracing::warn!` and fall back to the literal.
  Both warning paths use structured `event = "pi.models.api_key_env_*"`
  fields so they surface in operator dashboards.

Tests (all in the existing `mod tests`):
- `looks_like_api_key_env_var_accepts_typical_names`: DASHSCOPE_API_KEY,
  OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, PROVIDER42_API_KEY.
- `looks_like_api_key_env_var_rejects_non_matches`: wrong suffix, lowercase
  letters, real `sk-` keys, bare `_API_KEY`, leading digit.
- `resolve_value_resolves_bare_api_key_env_var_when_set`: happy path returns
  the resolved secret and asserts the lookup gets the right var name.
- `resolve_value_trims_whitespace_from_resolved_env_value`.
- `resolve_value_falls_back_to_literal_when_referenced_env_var_unset`.
- `resolve_value_falls_back_to_literal_when_referenced_env_var_empty`.
- `resolve_value_treats_literal_key_unchanged`: panics if the env_lookup
  closure is invoked for a literal-shaped key, proving the suffix gate
  short-circuits before any env access.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
